response: Robert M. Fomon was an American financier who served as the Chairman and Chief Executive Officer of E. F. Hutton & Co. from 1970 to 1987, and he was also the Governor of the New York Stock Exchange. Additionally, he held the position of Chairman of the Board of Governors of the Pacific Stock Exchange. The New York Stock Exchange, located at 11 Wall Street, Lower Manhattan, New York City, was under his leadership and was a significant part of his career and influence.
